serving.natural_language_portion: One whole Tartufo pizza, approximately 300 g, typically served as a single-person portion in an Italian restaurant setting.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza, cheese, regular crust (USDA SR Legacy, NDB 21299) and component estimation for truffle pizza
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for black truffle pizza exists in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Singapore Foodo. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy 'Pizza, cheese, regular crust' (NDB 21299) as the closest defensible analogue for a standard Italian-style cheese pizza. The addition of black truffle spread, quail eggs, and mushrooms modestly increases protein and fat; values are adjusted upward by approximately 5–8% for protein and fat to reflect these premium toppings. This is an estimated composition. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for black truffle pizza. GI of 50 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for standard cheese pizza (GI ~50, per Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables). The crust is the primary glycemic driver; truffle spread, eggs, and mushrooms do not materially alter GI. gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 × 94.5) / 100 = 47.3, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 31.5 × 3 = 94.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~560 mg per 100 g; ~1680 mg per whole pizza serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Tartufo pizza consumption due to its high sodium content from tomato sauce, mozzarella, and truffle spread; a single serving may provide up to 70–84% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~50) but high glycemic load per serving (~47) due to large refined-carbohydrate crust
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with Tartufo pizza as the large refined-flour crust produces a high glycemic load per serving (~47 g GL), which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation despite the moderate GI; portion control to half a pizza and pairing with a salad is advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from mozzarella cheese and quail eggs (~4.5 g per 100 g) and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume Tartufo pizza in moderation, as the combination of saturated fat from mozzarella and quail eggs and high sodium from sauce and cheese may adversely affect LDL cholesterol and blood pressure; limiting intake to occasional consumption and choosing smaller portions is recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tartufo pizza is a premium Italian-style pizza topped with black truffle spread (tartufo nero), quail eggs, mushrooms, tomato sauce, and mozzarella cheese, baked on a thin or regular wheat-flour crust. The name 'tartufo' is Italian for truffle, and this pizza is considered a luxury variant of classic Italian pizza due to the use of black truffle, a highly prized and aromatic fungus. The flavour profile is earthy, umami-rich, and savoury, with the truffle imparting a distinctive musky aroma that complements the creamy mozzarella and the richness of quail eggs. Mushrooms add additional umami depth. In Singapore, this dish is served at upscale Italian restaurants such as Ricciotti at The Riverwalk, typically priced at approximately SGD 30. Nutritionally, Tartufo pizza is a calorie-dense, carbohydrate-dominant dish (~266 kcal per 100 g, ~31.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g) with moderate protein (~11.5 g) and fat (~10.8 g) per 100 g. The sodium content is high (~560 mg per 100 g), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 50 (low GI), but the glycemic load per full serving is high (~47), which is clinically relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management. The saturated fat content (~4.5 g per 100 g) from mozzarella and eggs warrants moderation for cardiovascular health. This dish contains gluten, dairy, and eggs as major allergens.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry for black truffle pizza exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cheese pizza (NDB 21299) with component-based adjustments for truffle spread, quail eggs, and mushrooms. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database analogue for cheese pizza. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy 'Pizza, cheese, regular crust' (NDB 21299) adapted for the specific toppings (black truffle spread, quail eggs, mushrooms, tomato sauce, mozzarella). No exact match exists in any approved primary source. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry used for this estimation. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry used for this estimation. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry used for this estimation. 5. GI of 50 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database cheese pizza data; gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule. 6. The food_group 'Grains and staples' is preserved from the input as pizza is a grain-based dish. 7. Halal status is marked false as the restaurant (Ricciotti) is not listed as halal-certified in Singapore. 8. This is an Italian cuisine dish; multilingual naming rules for Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, and Vietnamese dishes do not apply. No mandatory non-English local names are required for Italian cuisine per PROJECT_VERITAS_Ver2.md Section 7.2. 9. The input food_name 'Tartufo' has been expanded to 'Tartufo Pizza' for canonical clarity, with 'Tartufo' retained as an alias.
